rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Use assign statements only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the analyzed model.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE annexes are visible in the current AADL component scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`
Implementation contracts use assign for binding to output features or auxiliary variables
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for guarantees.
Do not reference AADL property names like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: Boolean data range comparison and event port encoding
